skill.name: Payment Integration
skill.description: Integrate Stripe, PayPal, and payment processors. Handles checkout flows, subscriptions, webhooks, and PCI compliance.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for secure payment integration with Stripe, PayPal, and Square APIs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only instructional skill with no executable code. Static analysis found 0 security issues with a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides security best practices for payment integration including webhook signature verification, PCI compliance guidance, and idempotency patterns. No malicious patterns detected.

content.value_statement: Building payment systems requires careful attention to security and edge cases. This skill provides proven patterns for Stripe, PayPal, and Square integration with PCI compliance built-in.
actual_capabilities: Generate Stripe, PayPal, and Square API integration code with proper error handling
Implement webhook endpoints with signature verification and idempotency checks
Design database schemas for payment records and transaction history
Provide PCI compliance checklists and security best practices
Create test scenarios covering edge cases like failed payments and disputes
Configure environment variables for test and production payment gateways
limitations: Cannot execute payment API calls directly - provides code and guidance only
Requires developer to implement and test payment integrations in their environment
Does not replace formal PCI DSS certification or legal compliance review
Payment provider API changes may require updates to generated code

use_cases.title: E-commerce Checkout Integration
use_cases.description: Build secure checkout flows with Stripe Elements or PayPal buttons, including cart total validation and order confirmation webhooks.

use_cases.title: Subscription Billing System
use_cases.description: Implement recurring payment logic with proper handling of trial periods, proration, failed payment retries, and cancellation flows.

use_cases.title: Payment Webhook Handler
use_cases.description: Create robust webhook endpoints that verify signatures, ensure idempotency, and handle payment events like successful charges and disputes.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers integrating asynchronous payment notifications

prompt_templates.title: Basic Stripe Checkout Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Stripe checkout integration for my e-commerce site. I need a payment form using Stripe Elements, server-side payment intent creation, and webhook handling for successful payments.

prompt_templates.title: Subscription with Trial Period
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to implement a subscription plan with a 14-day free trial using Stripe Billing. Show me how to create the subscription, handle the trial period, and process the first payment when the trial ends.

prompt_templates.title: Webhook Security Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a secure webhook endpoint for Stripe that verifies signatures, checks for duplicate events, and handles payment_intent.succeeded and charge.disputed events. Include database storage for processed event IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Provider Payment Gateway
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to support both Stripe and PayPal checkout options. Design an abstraction layer that allows switching between providers, with unified payment confirmation handling and error reporting.

output_examples.input: Create a Stripe checkout session for a product with ID prod_123 and price $49.99
output_examples.output: Server-side code creating a Stripe Checkout Session with line items, success URL, cancel URL, and metadata for order tracking. Returns session ID for client redirect.

output_examples.input: How do I verify Stripe webhook signatures in Node.js?
output_examples.output: Code example using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent with the raw request body, signature header, and webhook secret. Includes error handling for invalid signatures and explanation of why JSON middleware must be bypassed.

best_practices: Always verify webhook signatures using official SDK methods before processing any payment events
Implement idempotency by storing processed event IDs to prevent duplicate order fulfillment
Use tokenization (Stripe Elements, PayPal SDK) to avoid handling raw card data and reduce PCI scope
anti_patterns: Never store raw credit card numbers, CVV codes, or full magnetic stripe data in your database
Do not skip webhook signature verification even in development environments
Avoid processing webhooks synchronously for expensive operations - return 200 OK quickly then process asynchronously

faq.question: What payment processors does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides integration guidance for Stripe, PayPal, and Square. Stripe is recommended for most use cases due to excellent documentation and developer experience.

faq.question: Do I need PCI compliance to use this skill?
faq.answer: When using tokenization (Stripe Elements, PayPal SDK), your PCI scope is significantly reduced to SAQ-A. You still need basic compliance but do not handle raw card data directly.

faq.question: How do I test payment integrations without charging real cards?
faq.answer: All payment providers offer test mode with test API keys and test card numbers. Use test mode during development and only switch to live keys when ready for production.

faq.question: What happens if a webhook is delivered multiple times?
faq.answer: Payment providers retry webhooks that do not receive a 2xx response. Implement idempotency by storing event IDs and checking before processing to prevent duplicate orders.

faq.question: Can this skill handle refunds and disputes?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides code patterns for processing refunds through payment provider APIs and handling dispute webhooks with proper notification and record keeping.

faq.question: How do I handle subscription plan changes and proration?
faq.answer: The skill generates code for Stripe Billing subscription updates with proration behavior configuration, including immediate charges or credits at next billing cycle.
